title: Astronomers
description: Observe, research, and interpret astronomical phenomena to increase basic knowledge or apply such information to practical problems.

tasks.text: Analyze research data to determine its significance, using computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Present research findings at scientific conferences and in papers written for scientific journals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study celestial phenomena, using a variety of ground-based and space-borne telescopes and scientific instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collaborate with other astronomers to carry out research projects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mentor graduate students and junior colleagues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise students' research on celestial and astronomical phenomena.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop theories based on personal observations or on observations and theories of other astronomers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure radio, infrared, gamma, and x-ray emissions from extraterrestrial sources.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop instrumentation and software for astronomical observation and analysis.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review scientific proposals and research papers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and modify astronomy-related programs for public presentation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Serve on professional panels and committees.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate orbits and determine sizes, shapes, brightness, and motions of different celestial bodies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct question-and-answer presentations on astronomy topics with public audiences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct the operations of a planetarium.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
